subject: Supplier invoice
snippet: Hey, I was reconciling the bakery accounts tonight and noticed the flour supplie...
body:
Hey, I was reconciling the bakery accounts tonight and noticed the flour supplier billed us twice for the same delivery on the 14th — once under invoice #4471 and again under #4483. Same amount, same order number. Can you check the account before we pay it again?

I didn't want to just ignore it in case it's actually two separate orders, but the dates line up too perfectly to be a coincidence.

subject: Re: Supplier invoice
snippet: Good catch. I pulled up the delivery log and it's definitely a duplicate — only ...
body:
Good catch. I pulled up the delivery log and it's definitely a duplicate — only one truck came that day. I'll call their billing office tomorrow morning and get #4483 voided before it hits the account.

Thanks for staying on top of this, you always catch the stuff I'd miss.

subject: Staffing schedule
snippet: David, quick heads up — Maria asked to swap off this Saturday for a family thing...
body:
David, quick heads up — Maria asked to swap off this Saturday for a family thing, and I don't have anyone else trained on the early proofing shift. Can we shuffle the schedule or do you want me to just cover it myself?

Let me know by tomorrow so I can post the updated board for the week.

subject: Re: Staffing schedule
snippet: Don't cover it yourself, you need the day off too. Let's move Danny to Saturday ...
body:
Don't cover it yourself, you need the day off too. Let's move Danny to Saturday morning since he shadowed you last month, and push Maria to next Tuesday's closing shift instead to even things out.

Go ahead and post it once Danny confirms he's good with the early start.

subject: Re: Staffing schedule
snippet: Danny's confirmed, he's actually excited to run the proofer solo. Schedule's pos...
body:
Danny's confirmed, he's actually excited to run the proofer solo. Schedule's posted for the week. I'll keep an eye on him Saturday morning just in case but I think he'll be fine.

Thanks for sorting it out fast.

subject: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Reminder that the Q2 estimated tax payment is due on the 15th. I ran the numbers...
body:
Reminder that the Q2 estimated tax payment is due on the 15th. I ran the numbers off the bakery's P&L through May and it looks like we should send around $4,200 to stay ahead of underpayment penalties. Can you confirm that matches what you're seeing?

I want to submit it a few days early this time instead of scrambling at the deadline like last quarter.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: That tracks with my numbers too, maybe a touch higher since May was stronger tha...
body:
That tracks with my numbers too, maybe a touch higher since May was stronger than April. I'd say round up to $4,400 just to be safe, especially with the new mixer purchase changing our depreciation this year.

Go ahead and submit whenever you're ready, I trust your math more than mine anyway.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Good point on the mixer, I forgot that would affect this quarter's deduction. On...
body:
Good point on the mixer, I forgot that would affect this quarter's deduction. One more question — should we count the catering deposits we've collected for July, or hold those until next quarter since the work hasn't happened yet?

Want to make sure we're not overstating income for a job we haven't delivered.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Hold those until next quarter, the accountant said last year that deposits count...
body:
Hold those until next quarter, the accountant said last year that deposits count when the service is rendered, not when cash comes in. So $4,400 for this round sounds right.

Thanks for double checking, this is exactly the kind of thing I'd forget.

subject: Customer review reply
snippet: We got a rough one-star review this morning saying the sourdough was underbaked ...
body:
We got a rough one-star review this morning saying the sourdough was underbaked and the person waited 20 minutes at the counter. I don't recognize the order but wanted to loop you in before I respond publicly. How do you want to handle it?

Here's the review text if you want to see it before I draft anything.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Let's respond quickly and own it without getting defensive. Something like: 'We'...
body:
Let's respond quickly and own it without getting defensive. Something like: 'We're sorry your visit didn't meet expectations — underbaked loaves and long waits aren't the norm here. We'd love to make it right, please email us directly.' Feel free to tweak the tone.

Also check if we had a wait-staff callout that morning, that might explain the delay.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: We did, actually — Priya called in sick and we were short one register person un...
body:
We did, actually — Priya called in sick and we were short one register person until 10am. I like your draft, maybe just soften 'aren't the norm here' to 'isn't what we aim for' so it doesn't sound defensive either.

Want me to post it as-is with that change?

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Yes, that's better, go ahead and post it. If they email us directly, offer a gif...
body:
Yes, that's better, go ahead and post it. If they email us directly, offer a gift card for a fresh loaf and an apology — no need to ask me first on that, just use your judgment.

Good catch on the tone, that's the kind of thing that turns a bad review into a repeat customer.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Posted it. Already got a reply notification, sounds like the review might be one...
body:
Posted it. Already got a reply notification, sounds like the review might be one of ours since the customer mentioned Priya by name in the response thread — small world. I offered the gift card, should hear back soon.

Will update you if it turns into anything worth following up on.

subject: Lunch this week?
snippet: It's been way too long since we grabbed lunch! I've got a light week at the shop...
body:
It's been way too long since we grabbed lunch! I've got a light week at the shop — Wednesday or Thursday work for you? There's that new noodle place on Charles Street I've been dying to try.

No pressure if the bakery's slammed, just say the word.

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: Thursday works great, Jake's got the shop covered that afternoon. Noodle place s...
body:
Thursday works great, Jake's got the shop covered that afternoon. Noodle place sounds perfect, I've heard good things. Let's do 12:30?

Can't wait to catch up, it really has been too long.

subject: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Hey stranger, I know Tuesdays are usually your slower day at the bakery. Want to...
body:
Hey stranger, I know Tuesdays are usually your slower day at the bakery. Want to grab coffee at that place near the flower shop around 9am before we both get swallowed by the day? I miss our chats.

Let me know if that time's no good and we'll find another.

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: 9am works, I'll swing by after I get the shop opened up. Honestly could use the break...
body:
9am works, I'll swing by after I get the shop opened up. Honestly could use the break, it's been a chaotic couple weeks between the schedule shuffle and a new supplier mess.

See you there, save me a seat by the window.

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Window seat secured. Bring the supplier drama, I've got plenty of flower deliver...
body:
Window seat secured. Bring the supplier drama, I've got plenty of flower delivery horror stories to trade. It'll be good to actually sit down instead of texting between errands.

See you Tuesday!

subject: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Okay I'm not letting your birthday slide by quietly this year. I was thinking br...
body:
Okay I'm not letting your birthday slide by quietly this year. I was thinking brunch on Saturday at that place with the bottomless mimosas you liked last summer — I can handle the reservation if you just tell me a rough headcount.

Also tell me if the kids are coming, changes whether I book the patio or the quieter back room.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: You're too sweet for this, honestly a quiet coffee would've been fine. Kids will...
body:
You're too sweet for this, honestly a quiet coffee would've been fine. Kids will be there for the first hour then heading to my sister's, so maybe the back room works better early and we shift outside once they leave.

Headcount's probably eight, I'll confirm once I hear back from a couple people.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Back room to patio transition, got it, I'll ask if they can hold both. Eight sou...
body:
Back room to patio transition, got it, I'll ask if they can hold both. Eight sounds manageable for the reservation. Should I order a cake or are we keeping it low-key with just brunch food?

Also no arguing about the bill, this one's on me.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Fine, fine, I'll let you win this one. Skip the cake, we'll have plenty of pastr...
body:
Fine, fine, I'll let you win this one. Skip the cake, we'll have plenty of pastries from the shop that I can bring instead — least I can do. Saturday at 11 then?

Really, thank you for putting this together, means a lot.

subject: Renewal options
snippet: The liability insurance renewal notice came in for the bakery. They're offering ...
body:
The liability insurance renewal notice came in for the bakery. They're offering three tiers this year — the current plan renews at a 12% increase, or we could downgrade coverage slightly to keep the premium flat, or upgrade to add equipment breakdown coverage for about $60/month more.

Given the mixer repair scare last month, I'm leaning toward the upgrade. Want to look at the numbers together this weekend?

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Yeah let's do that. The mixer scare is fresh enough in my mind that $60/month fo...
body:
Yeah let's do that. The mixer scare is fresh enough in my mind that $60/month for equipment coverage feels cheap insurance, pun intended. Can you send over the actual quote sheet so I can see what's excluded?

Want to make sure things like the walk-in cooler are actually covered and not some carve-out.

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Attached the quote sheet — walk-in cooler is covered up to $8,000 replacement va...
body:
Attached the quote sheet — walk-in cooler is covered up to $8,000 replacement value, and the mixer and proofer are both listed by name under the equipment schedule. Only real exclusion is cosmetic damage, which makes sense.

The flat-premium downgrade option actually removes theft coverage for the register area, which seems risky given we're on a corner lot.

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Yeah, dropping theft coverage on the corner lot location sounds like a bad trade...
body:
Yeah, dropping theft coverage on the corner lot location sounds like a bad trade for saving a few bucks a month. Let's go with the upgrade tier — equipment coverage plus keeping theft protection feels like the right call for where we are right now.

Go ahead and lock it in if you're comfortable with the numbers.

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Agreed, I'll call them Monday morning and confirm the upgrade tier before the re...
body:
Agreed, I'll call them Monday morning and confirm the upgrade tier before the renewal deadline. Should lock in the rate for another 12 months once it's processed.

I'll forward the confirmation once it goes through so we both have it on file.

subject: Vendor invoice question
snippet: Quick one — the produce vendor's latest invoice has a $45 'fuel surcharge' line ...
body:
Quick one — the produce vendor's latest invoice has a $45 'fuel surcharge' line item I don't remember seeing before. Is this a new thing they added or did we miss a fee increase notice somewhere?

Not a huge deal either way, just want to know if it's a one-time thing or ongoing.

subject: Re: Vendor invoice question
snippet: They mentioned it in passing last delivery, said gas prices pushed them to add i...
body:
They mentioned it in passing last delivery, said gas prices pushed them to add it to all accounts starting this month. Should be ongoing, not a one-off. I'll ask if there's a way to reduce it by bundling our order days.

Otherwise it's small enough I wasn't going to push back too hard.

subject: Shop lease renewal
snippet: Landlord sent over the renewal terms — they want to bump rent by $150/month for ...
body:
Landlord sent over the renewal terms for the Atwells storefront — they want to bump the rent by $150/month for another 12-month term, or we can go month-to-month at a bigger increase. Renewal deadline is in three weeks.

Given everything else going on with the bakery, I'd rather lock in the 12-month term if we can, but wanted your take before I reply.

subject: Re: Shop lease renewal
snippet: $150 stings but honestly matches what I've seen for the other storefronts on Atwells lately. I'd ...
body:
$150 stings but honestly matches what I've seen for the other storefronts on Atwells lately. I'd rather have the stability of the 12-month lease too instead of month-to-month uncertainty on top of everything at the shop.

Can you ask if they'd meet in the middle at $100 given we've never been late on rent? Worth a shot before we just accept it.

subject: Re: Shop lease renewal
snippet: Asked, and they came back with $120 as a compromise for the 12-month term, said ...
body:
Asked, and they came back with $120 as a compromise for the 12-month term, said it's the lowest they can go company-wide this cycle. I think that's reasonable enough to just take.

I'll sign and send it back tomorrow unless you want to push further.

subject: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: This week's newsletter roundup had a couple good ones — an article on small bake...
body:
This week's newsletter roundup had a couple good ones — an article on small bakeries surviving flour price spikes by switching suppliers seasonally, and another on how local shops are using loyalty apps instead of punch cards. Thought both were relevant to us.

Linking them below in case you want to read on your lunch break.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: The loyalty app one is timely, I've been meaning to look into replacing our pape...
body:
The loyalty app one is timely, I've been meaning to look into replacing our paper punch cards anyway, half of them get lost before customers fill them out. Going to read the seasonal supplier piece tonight.

Thanks for flagging these, you find the good stuff way better than I do scrolling.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: Glad it's useful! There's also a shorter piece this week on a bakery up in Vermont...
body:
Glad it's useful! There's also a shorter piece this week on a bakery up in Vermont that started doing subscription bread boxes — weekly delivery for a flat fee. Might be worth a look if we ever want a steadier revenue stream in the slow months.

Not urgent, just filing it away for future brainstorming.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: That subscription box idea has been rattling around in my head too actually, mig...
body:
That subscription box idea has been rattling around in my head too actually, might bring it up at the next team meeting to see what Jake thinks about the logistics. Slow winter months could really use something like that.

Keep sending these, they're turning into a decent idea pipeline for us.

subject: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: Can we sort out Mom's rides this week? I've got a late delivery Wednesday so I ca...
body:
Can we sort out Mom's rides this week? I've got a late delivery Wednesday so I can't take her to the 3:15 appointment. Any chance you can grab her that day and I'll cover Thursday and Friday?

Monday and Tuesday I've already got covered like usual.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: Wednesday works, I can have Jake close up that afternoon so I'm free by 3. Thurs...
body:
Wednesday works, I can have Jake close up that afternoon so I'm free by 3. Thursday and Friday sound good on your end then. Should I also handle the dentist appointment Wednesday evening since I'll already be out?

Just let me know the time and I'll add it to the day.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: That would be a huge help, appointment's at 5pm at Dr. Patel's office, just her m...
body:
That would be a huge help, appointment's at 5pm at Dr. Patel's office, just her monthly check-in this time. Actually, one more wrinkle — Thursday might need to shift to 4pm instead of 3:15, her physical therapy got rescheduled.

Sorry for the moving pieces this week, it's been a lot.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: No worries at all, 4pm Thursday is fine, I'll just tell Jake I need a bit more c...
body:
No worries at all, 4pm Thursday is fine, I'll just tell Jake I need a bit more coverage that day. I've got Wednesday's dentist appointment written down too. We'll get through the week, it happens.

Let me know if Friday shifts too, otherwise I'll assume it's steady as planned.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: Friday's steady, no changes there. Thanks for being flexible on all this, I know...
body:
Friday's steady, no changes there. Thanks for being flexible on all this, I know the bakery schedule isn't exactly forgiving either. I owe you a coffee run at minimum.

Okay, I think we're all set for the week!

subject: Family vacation planning
snippet: I was looking at the calendar and the last week of August seems like the only re...
body:
I was looking at the calendar and the last week of August seems like the only real gap before your busy fall catering season kicks in. Should we start looking at the beach house again, or try somewhere new this year?

Want to lock in dates soon before decent places get booked up.

subject: Re: Family vacation planning
snippet: Last week of August works on my end too, I'll ask Jake to run the shop solo that...
body:
Last week of August works on my end too, I'll ask Jake to run the shop solo that week since he's been wanting more responsibility anyway. I'm honestly fine with the beach house again, Ryan and Emily loved it and it's easy to book.

Go ahead and check availability, I trust your pick either way.

subject: Weekend plans
snippet: Any thoughts on this weekend? I was thinking we could finally do that farmers ma...
body:
Any thoughts on this weekend? I was thinking we could finally do that farmers market Saturday morning before it gets too hot, then maybe just a low-key evening at home. Feels like we haven't had an unscheduled weekend in a while.

Open to other ideas too if you'd rather do something else.

subject: Re: Weekend plans
snippet: Farmers market sounds perfect actually, I could use some inspiration for the sho...
body:
Farmers market sounds perfect actually, I could use some inspiration for the shop anyway, always good to see what other vendors are doing. Low-key evening after works great too, I'm honestly a little wiped from this week.

Should we invite Karen along to the market or keep it low-key?

subject: Re: Weekend plans
snippet: Let's keep it just us this time, we can always do Karen next weekend. I'll pack ...
body:
Let's keep it just us this time, we can always do Karen next weekend. I'll pack a small cooler bag so we're not rushing home right after. Saturday morning it is then.

Looking forward to a slow one for once.

subject: Supplier invoice
snippet: Hey, I was reconciling the bakery accounts tonight and noticed the flour supplie...
body:
Hey, I was reconciling the bakery accounts tonight and noticed the flour supplier billed us twice for the same delivery on the 14th — once under invoice #4471 and again under #4483. Same amount, same order number. Can you check the account before we pay it again?

I didn't want to just ignore it in case it's actually two separate orders, but the dates line up too perfectly to be a coincidence.

subject: Re: Supplier invoice
snippet: Good catch. I pulled up the delivery log and it's definitely a duplicate — only ...
body:
Good catch. I pulled up the delivery log and it's definitely a duplicate — only one truck came that day. I'll call their billing office tomorrow morning and get #4483 voided before it hits the account.

Thanks for staying on top of this, you always catch the stuff I'd miss.

subject: Staffing schedule
snippet: David, quick heads up — Maria asked to swap off this Saturday for a family thing...
body:
David, quick heads up — Maria asked to swap off this Saturday for a family thing, and I don't have anyone else trained on the early proofing shift. Can we shuffle the schedule or do you want me to just cover it myself?

Let me know by tomorrow so I can post the updated board for the week.

subject: Re: Staffing schedule
snippet: Don't cover it yourself, you need the day off too. Let's move Danny to Saturday ...
body:
Don't cover it yourself, you need the day off too. Let's move Danny to Saturday morning since he shadowed you last month, and push Maria to next Tuesday's closing shift instead to even things out.

Go ahead and post it once Danny confirms he's good with the early start.

subject: Re: Staffing schedule
snippet: Danny's confirmed, he's actually excited to run the proofer solo. Schedule's pos...
body:
Danny's confirmed, he's actually excited to run the proofer solo. Schedule's posted for the week. I'll keep an eye on him Saturday morning just in case but I think he'll be fine.

Thanks for sorting it out fast.

subject: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Reminder that the Q2 estimated tax payment is due on the 15th. I ran the numbers...
body:
Reminder that the Q2 estimated tax payment is due on the 15th. I ran the numbers off the bakery's P&L through May and it looks like we should send around $4,200 to stay ahead of underpayment penalties. Can you confirm that matches what you're seeing?

I want to submit it a few days early this time instead of scrambling at the deadline like last quarter.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: That tracks with my numbers too, maybe a touch higher since May was stronger tha...
body:
That tracks with my numbers too, maybe a touch higher since May was stronger than April. I'd say round up to $4,400 just to be safe, especially with the new mixer purchase changing our depreciation this year.

Go ahead and submit whenever you're ready, I trust your math more than mine anyway.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Good point on the mixer, I forgot that would affect this quarter's deduction. On...
body:
Good point on the mixer, I forgot that would affect this quarter's deduction. One more question — should we count the catering deposits we've collected for July, or hold those until next quarter since the work hasn't happened yet?

Want to make sure we're not overstating income for a job we haven't delivered.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Hold those until next quarter, the accountant said last year that deposits count...
body:
Hold those until next quarter, the accountant said last year that deposits count when the service is rendered, not when cash comes in. So $4,400 for this round sounds right.

Thanks for double checking, this is exactly the kind of thing I'd forget.

subject: Customer review reply
snippet: We got a rough one-star review this morning saying the sourdough was underbaked ...
body:
We got a rough one-star review this morning saying the sourdough was underbaked and the person waited 20 minutes at the counter. I don't recognize the order but wanted to loop you in before I respond publicly. How do you want to handle it?

Here's the review text if you want to see it before I draft anything.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Let's respond quickly and own it without getting defensive. Something like: 'We'...
body:
Let's respond quickly and own it without getting defensive. Something like: 'We're sorry your visit didn't meet expectations — underbaked loaves and long waits aren't the norm here. We'd love to make it right, please email us directly.' Feel free to tweak the tone.

Also check if we had a wait-staff callout that morning, that might explain the delay.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: We did, actually — Priya called in sick and we were short one register person un...
body:
We did, actually — Priya called in sick and we were short one register person until 10am. I like your draft, maybe just soften 'aren't the norm here' to 'isn't what we aim for' so it doesn't sound defensive either.

Want me to post it as-is with that change?

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Yes, that's better, go ahead and post it. If they email us directly, offer a gif...
body:
Yes, that's better, go ahead and post it. If they email us directly, offer a gift card for a fresh loaf and an apology — no need to ask me first on that, just use your judgment.

Good catch on the tone, that's the kind of thing that turns a bad review into a repeat customer.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Posted it. Already got a reply notification, sounds like the review might be one...
body:
Posted it. Already got a reply notification, sounds like the review might be one of ours since the customer mentioned Priya by name in the response thread — small world. I offered the gift card, should hear back soon.

Will update you if it turns into anything worth following up on.

subject: Lunch this week?
snippet: It's been way too long since we grabbed lunch! I've got a light week at the shop...
body:
It's been way too long since we grabbed lunch! I've got a light week at the shop — Wednesday or Thursday work for you? There's that new noodle place on Charles Street I've been dying to try.

No pressure if the bakery's slammed, just say the word.

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: Thursday works great, Jake's got the shop covered that afternoon. Noodle place s...
body:
Thursday works great, Jake's got the shop covered that afternoon. Noodle place sounds perfect, I've heard good things. Let's do 12:30?

Can't wait to catch up, it really has been too long.

snippet: Hey stranger, I know Tuesdays are usually your slower day at the bakery. Want to...
body:
Hey stranger, I know Tuesdays are usually your slower day at the bakery. Want to grab coffee at that place near the flower shop around 9am before we both get swallowed by the day? I miss our chats.

Let me know if that time's no good and we'll find another.

snippet: 9am works, I'll swing by after I get the shop opened up. Honestly could use the break...
body:

See you there, save me a seat by the window.

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Window seat secured. Bring the supplier drama, I've got plenty of flower deliver...
body:
Window seat secured. Bring the supplier drama, I've got plenty of flower delivery horror stories to trade. It'll be good to actually sit down instead of texting between errands.

See you Tuesday!

snippet: Okay I'm not letting your birthday slide by quietly this year. I was thinking br...
body:

Also tell me if the kids are coming, changes whether I book the patio or the quieter back room.

snippet: You're too sweet for this, honestly a quiet coffee would've been fine. Kids will...
body:

Headcount's probably eight, I'll confirm once I hear back from a couple people.

snippet: Back room to patio transition, got it, I'll ask if they can hold both. Eight sou...

Also no arguing about the bill, this one's on me.

snippet: Fine, fine, I'll let you win this one. Skip the cake, we'll have plenty of pastr...

Given the mixer repair scare last month, I'm leaning toward the upgrade. Want to look at the numbers together this weekend?

snippet: Yeah let's do that. The mixer scare is fresh enough in my mind that $60/month fo...
body:

Want to make sure things like the walk-in cooler are actually covered and not some carve-out.

subject: Vendor invoice question

Quick one — the produce vendor's latest invoice has a $45 'fuel surcharge' line item I don't remember seeing before. Is this a new thing they added or did we miss a fee increase notice somewhere?

Not a huge deal either way, just want to know if it's a one-time thing or ongoing.

subject: Re: Vendor invoice question
snippet: They mentioned it in passing last delivery, said gas prices pushed them to add i...
body:
They mentioned it in passing last delivery, said gas prices pushed them to add it to all accounts starting this month. Should be ongoing, not a one-off. I'll ask if there's a way to reduce it by bundling our order days.

Otherwise it's small enough I wasn't going to push back too hard.

subject: Shop lease renewal
snippet: Landlord sent over the renewal terms — they want to bump rent by $150/month for ...
body:
Landlord sent over the renewal terms for the Atwells storefront — they want to bump the rent by $150/month for another 12-month term, or we can go month-to-month at a bigger increase. Renewal deadline is in three weeks.

Given everything else going on with the bakery, I'd rather lock in the 12-month term if we can, but wanted your take before I reply.

subject: Re: Shop lease renewal
snippet: $150 stings but honestly matches what I've seen for the other storefronts on Atwells lately. I'd ...
body:
$150 stings but honestly matches what I've seen for the other storefronts on Atwells lately. I'd rather have the stability of the 12-month lease too instead of month-to-month uncertainty on top of everything at the shop.

Can you ask if they'd meet in the middle at $100 given we've never been late on rent? Worth a shot before we just accept it.

subject: Re: Shop lease renewal
snippet: Asked, and they came back with $120 as a compromise for the 12-month term, said ...
body:
Asked, and they came back with $120 as a compromise for the 12-month term, said it's the lowest they can go company-wide this cycle. I think that's reasonable enough to just take.

I'll sign and send it back tomorrow unless you want to push further.

subject: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: This week's newsletter roundup had a couple good ones — an article on small bake...
body:
This week's newsletter roundup had a couple good ones — an article on small bakeries surviving flour price spikes by switching suppliers seasonally, and another on how local shops are using loyalty apps instead of punch cards. Thought both were relevant to us.

Linking them below in case you want to read on your lunch break.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: The loyalty app one is timely, I've been meaning to look into replacing our pape...
body:
The loyalty app one is timely, I've been meaning to look into replacing our paper punch cards anyway, half of them get lost before customers fill them out. Going to read the seasonal supplier piece tonight.

Thanks for flagging these, you find the good stuff way better than I do scrolling.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: Glad it's useful! There's also a shorter piece this week on a bakery up in Vermont...
body:
Glad it's useful! There's also a shorter piece this week on a bakery up in Vermont that started doing subscription bread boxes — weekly delivery for a flat fee. Might be worth a look if we ever want a steadier revenue stream in the slow months.

Not urgent, just filing it away for future brainstorming.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: That subscription box idea has been rattling around in my head too actually, mig...
body:
That subscription box idea has been rattling around in my head too actually, might bring it up at the next team meeting to see what Jake thinks about the logistics. Slow winter months could really use something like that.

Keep sending these, they're turning into a decent idea pipeline for us.

subject: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: Can we sort out Mom's rides this week? I've got a late delivery Wednesday so I ca...
body:
Can we sort out Mom's rides this week? I've got a late delivery Wednesday so I can't take her to the 3:15 appointment. Any chance you can grab her that day and I'll cover Thursday and Friday?

Monday and Tuesday I've already got covered like usual.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: Wednesday works, I can have Jake close up that afternoon so I'm free by 3. Thurs...
body:
Wednesday works, I can have Jake close up that afternoon so I'm free by 3. Thursday and Friday sound good on your end then. Should I also handle the dentist appointment Wednesday evening since I'll already be out?

Just let me know the time and I'll add it to the day.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: That would be a huge help, appointment's at 5pm at Dr. Patel's office, just her m...
body:
That would be a huge help, appointment's at 5pm at Dr. Patel's office, just her monthly check-in this time. Actually, one more wrinkle — Thursday might need to shift to 4pm instead of 3:15, her physical therapy got rescheduled.

Sorry for the moving pieces this week, it's been a lot.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: No worries at all, 4pm Thursday is fine, I'll just tell Jake I need a bit more c...
body:
No worries at all, 4pm Thursday is fine, I'll just tell Jake I need a bit more coverage that day. I've got Wednesday's dentist appointment written down too. We'll get through the week, it happens.

Let me know if Friday shifts too, otherwise I'll assume it's steady as planned.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: Friday's steady, no changes there. Thanks for being flexible on all this, I know...
body:
Friday's steady, no changes there. Thanks for being flexible on all this, I know the bakery schedule isn't exactly forgiving either. I owe you a coffee run at minimum.

Okay, I think we're all set for the week!

subject: Family vacation planning
snippet: I was looking at the calendar and the last week of August seems like the only re...
body:
I was looking at the calendar and the last week of August seems like the only real gap before your busy fall catering season kicks in. Should we start looking at the beach house again, or try somewhere new this year?

Want to lock in dates soon before decent places get booked up.

subject: Re: Family vacation planning
snippet: Last week of August works on my end too, I'll ask Jake to run the shop solo that...
body:
Last week of August works on my end too, I'll ask Jake to run the shop solo that week since he's been wanting more responsibility anyway. I'm honestly fine with the beach house again, Ryan and Emily loved it and it's easy to book.

Go ahead and check availability, I trust your pick either way.

subject: Weekend plans
snippet: Any thoughts on this weekend? I was thinking we could finally do that farmers ma...
body:
Any thoughts on this weekend? I was thinking we could finally do that farmers market Saturday morning before it gets too hot, then maybe just a low-key evening at home. Feels like we haven't had an unscheduled weekend in a while.

Open to other ideas too if you'd rather do something else.

subject: Re: Weekend plans
snippet: Farmers market sounds perfect actually, I could use some inspiration for the sho...
body:
Farmers market sounds perfect actually, I could use some inspiration for the shop anyway, always good to see what other vendors are doing. Low-key evening after works great too, I'm honestly a little wiped from this week.

Should we invite Karen along to the market or keep it low-key?

subject: Re: Weekend plans
snippet: Let's keep it just us this time, we can always do Karen next weekend. I'll pack ...
body:
Let's keep it just us this time, we can always do Karen next weekend. I'll pack a small cooler bag so we're not rushing home right after. Saturday morning it is then.

Looking forward to a slow one for once.

subject: Supplier invoice
snippet: Hey, I was reconciling the bakery accounts tonight and noticed the flour supplie...
body:
Hey, I was reconciling the bakery accounts tonight and noticed the flour supplier billed us twice for the same delivery on the 14th — once under invoice #4471 and again under #4483. Same amount, same order number. Can you check the account before we pay it again?

I didn't want to just ignore it in case it's actually two separate orders, but the dates line up too perfectly to be a coincidence.

subject: Re: Supplier invoice
snippet: Good catch. I pulled up the delivery log and it's definitely a duplicate — only ...
body:
Good catch. I pulled up the delivery log and it's definitely a duplicate — only one truck came that day. I'll call their billing office tomorrow morning and get #4483 voided before it hits the account.

Thanks for staying on top of this, you always catch the stuff I'd miss.

subject: Staffing schedule
snippet: David, quick heads up — Maria asked to swap off this Saturday for a family thing...
body:
David, quick heads up — Maria asked to swap off this Saturday for a family thing, and I don't have anyone else trained on the early proofing shift. Can we shuffle the schedule or do you want me to just cover it myself?

Let me know by tomorrow so I can post the updated board for the week.

subject: Re: Staffing schedule
snippet: Don't cover it yourself, you need the day off too. Let's move Danny to Saturday ...
body:
Don't cover it yourself, you need the day off too. Let's move Danny to Saturday morning since he shadowed you last month, and push Maria to next Tuesday's closing shift instead to even things out.

Go ahead and post it once Danny confirms he's good with the early start.

subject: Re: Staffing schedule
snippet: Danny's confirmed, he's actually excited to run the proofer solo. Schedule's pos...
body:
Danny's confirmed, he's actually excited to run the proofer solo. Schedule's posted for the week. I'll keep an eye on him Saturday morning just in case but I think he'll be fine.

Thanks for sorting it out fast.

subject: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Reminder that the Q2 estimated tax payment is due on the 15th. I ran the numbers...
body:
Reminder that the Q2 estimated tax payment is due on the 15th. I ran the numbers off the bakery's P&L through May and it looks like we should send around $4,200 to stay ahead of underpayment penalties. Can you confirm that matches what you're seeing?

I want to submit it a few days early this time instead of scrambling at the deadline like last quarter.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: That tracks with my numbers too, maybe a touch higher since May was stronger tha...
body:
That tracks with my numbers too, maybe a touch higher since May was stronger than April. I'd say round up to $4,400 just to be safe, especially with the new mixer purchase changing our depreciation this year.

Go ahead and submit whenever you're ready, I trust your math more than mine anyway.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Good point on the mixer, I forgot that would affect this quarter's deduction. On...
body:
Good point on the mixer, I forgot that would affect this quarter's deduction. One more question — should we count the catering deposits we've collected for July, or hold those until next quarter since the work hasn't happened yet?

Want to make sure we're not overstating income for a job we haven't delivered.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Hold those until next quarter, the accountant said last year that deposits count...
body:
Hold those until next quarter, the accountant said last year that deposits count when the service is rendered, not when cash comes in. So $4,400 for this round sounds right.

Thanks for double checking, this is exactly the kind of thing I'd forget.

subject: Customer review reply
snippet: We got a rough one-star review this morning saying the sourdough was underbaked ...
body:
We got a rough one-star review this morning saying the sourdough was underbaked and the person waited 20 minutes at the counter. I don't recognize the order but wanted to loop you in before I respond publicly. How do you want to handle it?

Here's the review text if you want to see it before I draft anything.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Let's respond quickly and own it without getting defensive. Something like: 'We'...
body:
Let's respond quickly and own it without getting defensive. Something like: 'We're sorry your visit didn't meet expectations — underbaked loaves and long waits aren't the norm here. We'd love to make it right, please email us directly.' Feel free to tweak the tone.

Also check if we had a wait-staff callout that morning, that might explain the delay.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: We did, actually — Priya called in sick and we were short one register person un...
body:
We did, actually — Priya called in sick and we were short one register person until 10am. I like your draft, maybe just soften 'aren't the norm here' to 'isn't what we aim for' so it doesn't sound defensive either.

Want me to post it as-is with that change?

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Yes, that's better, go ahead and post it. If they email us directly, offer a gif...
body:
Yes, that's better, go ahead and post it. If they email us directly, offer a gift card for a fresh loaf and an apology — no need to ask me first on that, just use your judgment.

Good catch on the tone, that's the kind of thing that turns a bad review into a repeat customer.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Posted it. Already got a reply notification, sounds like the review might be one...
body:
Posted it. Already got a reply notification, sounds like the review might be one of ours since the customer mentioned Priya by name in the response thread — small world. I offered the gift card, should hear back soon.

Will update you if it turns into anything worth following up on.

subject: Lunch this week?
snippet: It's been way too long since we grabbed lunch! I've got a light week at the shop...
body:
It's been way too long since we grabbed lunch! I've got a light week at the shop — Wednesday or Thursday work for you? There's that new noodle place on Charles Street I've been dying to try.

No pressure if the bakery's slammed, just say the word.

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: Thursday works great, Jake's got the shop covered that afternoon. Noodle place s...
body:
Thursday works great, Jake's got the shop covered that afternoon. Noodle place sounds perfect, I've heard good things. Let's do 12:30?

Can't wait to catch up, it really has been too long.

subject: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Hey stranger, I know Tuesdays are usually your slower day at the bakery. Want to...
body:
Hey stranger, I know Tuesdays are usually your slower day at the bakery. Want to grab coffee at that place near the flower shop around 9am before we both get swallowed by the day? I miss our chats.

Let me know if that time's no good and we'll find another.

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: 9am works, I'll swing by after I get the shop opened up. Honestly could use the break...
body:
9am works, I'll swing by after I get the shop opened up. Honestly could use the break, it's been a chaotic couple weeks between the schedule shuffle and a new supplier mess.

See you there, save me a seat by the window.

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Window seat secured. Bring the supplier drama, I've got plenty of flower deliver...
body:
Window seat secured. Bring the supplier drama, I've got plenty of flower delivery horror stories to trade. It'll be good to actually sit down instead of texting between errands.

See you Tuesday!

subject: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Okay I'm not letting your birthday slide by quietly this year. I was thinking br...
body:
Okay I'm not letting your birthday slide by quietly this year. I was thinking brunch on Saturday at that place with the bottomless mimosas you liked last summer — I can handle the reservation if you just tell me a rough headcount.

Also tell me if the kids are coming, changes whether I book the patio or the quieter back room.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: You're too sweet for this, honestly a quiet coffee would've been fine. Kids will...
body:
You're too sweet for this, honestly a quiet coffee would've been fine. Kids will be there for the first hour then heading to my sister's, so maybe the back room works better early and we shift outside once they leave.

Headcount's probably eight, I'll confirm once I hear back from a couple people.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Back room to patio transition, got it, I'll ask if they can hold both. Eight sou...
body:
Back room to patio transition, got it, I'll ask if they can hold both. Eight sounds manageable for the reservation. Should I order a cake or are we keeping it low-key with just brunch food?

Also no arguing about the bill, this one's on me.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Fine, fine, I'll let you win this one. Skip the cake, we'll have plenty of pastr...
body:
Fine, fine, I'll let you win this one. Skip the cake, we'll have plenty of pastries from the shop that I can bring instead — least I can do. Saturday at 11 then?

Really, thank you for putting this together, means a lot.

subject: Renewal options
snippet: The liability insurance renewal notice came in for the bakery. They're offering ...
body:
The liability insurance renewal notice came in for the bakery. They're offering three tiers this year — the current plan renews at a 12% increase, or we could downgrade coverage slightly to keep the premium flat, or upgrade to add equipment breakdown coverage for about $60/month more.

Given the mixer repair scare last month, I'm leaning toward the upgrade. Want to look at the numbers together this weekend?

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Yeah let's do that. The mixer scare is fresh enough in my mind that $60/month fo...
body:
Yeah let's do that. The mixer scare is fresh enough in my mind that $60/month for equipment coverage feels cheap insurance, pun intended. Can you send over the actual quote sheet so I can see what's excluded?

Want to make sure things like the walk-in cooler are actually covered and not some carve-out.

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Attached the quote sheet — walk-in cooler is covered up to $8,000 replacement va...
body:
Attached the quote sheet — walk-in cooler is covered up to $8,000 replacement value, and the mixer and proofer are both listed by name under the equipment schedule. Only real exclusion is cosmetic damage, which makes sense.

The flat-premium downgrade option actually removes theft coverage for the register area, which seems risky given we're on a corner lot.

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Yeah, dropping theft coverage on the corner lot location sounds like a bad trade...
body:
Yeah, dropping theft coverage on the corner lot location sounds like a bad trade for saving a few bucks a month. Let's go with the upgrade tier — equipment coverage plus keeping theft protection feels like the right call for where we are right now.

Go ahead and lock it in if you're comfortable with the numbers.

subject: Re: Renewal options
snippet: Agreed, I'll call them Monday morning and confirm the upgrade tier before the re...
body:
Agreed, I'll call them Monday morning and confirm the upgrade tier before the renewal deadline. Should lock in the rate for another 12 months once it's processed.

I'll forward the confirmation once it goes through so we both have it on file.

subject: Vendor invoice question
snippet: Quick one — the produce vendor's latest invoice has a $45 'fuel surcharge' line ...
body:
Quick one — the produce vendor's latest invoice has a $45 'fuel surcharge' line item I don't remember seeing before. Is this a new thing they added or did we miss a fee increase notice somewhere?

Not a huge deal either way, just want to know if it's a one-time thing or ongoing.

subject: Re: Vendor invoice question
snippet: They mentioned it in passing last delivery, said gas prices pushed them to add i...
body:
They mentioned it in passing last delivery, said gas prices pushed them to add it to all accounts starting this month. Should be ongoing, not a one-off. I'll ask if there's a way to reduce it by bundling our order days.

Otherwise it's small enough I wasn't going to push back too hard.

subject: Shop lease renewal
snippet: Landlord sent over the renewal terms — they want to bump rent by $150/month for ...
body:
Landlord sent over the renewal terms for the Atwells storefront — they want to bump the rent by $150/month for another 12-month term, or we can go month-to-month at a bigger increase. Renewal deadline is in three weeks.

Given everything else going on with the bakery, I'd rather lock in the 12-month term if we can, but wanted your take before I reply.

subject: Re: Shop lease renewal
snippet: $150 stings but honestly matches what I've seen for the other storefronts on Atwells lately. I'd ...
body:
$150 stings but honestly matches what I've seen for the other storefronts on Atwells lately. I'd rather have the stability of the 12-month lease too instead of month-to-month uncertainty on top of everything at the shop.

Can you ask if they'd meet in the middle at $100 given we've never been late on rent? Worth a shot before we just accept it.

subject: Re: Shop lease renewal
snippet: Asked, and they came back with $120 as a compromise for the 12-month term, said ...
body:
Asked, and they came back with $120 as a compromise for the 12-month term, said it's the lowest they can go company-wide this cycle. I think that's reasonable enough to just take.

I'll sign and send it back tomorrow unless you want to push further.

subject: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: This week's newsletter roundup had a couple good ones — an article on small bake...
body:
This week's newsletter roundup had a couple good ones — an article on small bakeries surviving flour price spikes by switching suppliers seasonally, and another on how local shops are using loyalty apps instead of punch cards. Thought both were relevant to us.

Linking them below in case you want to read on your lunch break.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: The loyalty app one is timely, I've been meaning to look into replacing our pape...
body:
The loyalty app one is timely, I've been meaning to look into replacing our paper punch cards anyway, half of them get lost before customers fill them out. Going to read the seasonal supplier piece tonight.

Thanks for flagging these, you find the good stuff way better than I do scrolling.

snippet: Glad it's useful! There's also a shorter piece this week on a bakery up in Vermont...
body:
Glad it's useful! There's also a shorter piece this week on a bakery up in Vermont that started doing subscription bread boxes — weekly delivery for a flat fee. Might be worth a look if we ever want a steadier revenue stream in the slow months.

Not urgent, just filing it away for future brainstorming.

subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
snippet: That subscription box idea has been rattling around in my head too actually, mig...
body:
That subscription box idea has been rattling around in my head too actually, might bring it up at the next team meeting to see what Jake thinks about the logistics. Slow winter months could really use something like that.

Keep sending these, they're turning into a decent idea pipeline for us.

subject: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: Can we sort out Mom's rides this week? I've got a late delivery Wednesday so I ca...
body:
Can we sort out Mom's rides this week? I've got a late delivery Wednesday so I can't take her to the 3:15 appointment. Any chance you can grab her that day and I'll cover Thursday and Friday?

Monday and Tuesday I've already got covered like usual.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: Wednesday works, I can have Jake close up that afternoon so I'm free by 3. Thurs...
body:
Wednesday works, I can have Jake close up that afternoon so I'm free by 3. Thursday and Friday sound good on your end then. Should I also handle the dentist appointment Wednesday evening since I'll already be out?

Just let me know the time and I'll add it to the day.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: That would be a huge help, appointment's at 5pm at Dr. Patel's office, just her m...
body:
That would be a huge help, appointment's at 5pm at Dr. Patel's office, just her monthly check-in this time. Actually, one more wrinkle — Thursday might need to shift to 4pm instead of 3:15, her physical therapy got rescheduled.

Sorry for the moving pieces this week, it's been a lot.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: No worries at all, 4pm Thursday is fine, I'll just tell Jake I need a bit more c...
body:
No worries at all, 4pm Thursday is fine, I'll just tell Jake I need a bit more coverage that day. I've got Wednesday's dentist appointment written down too. We'll get through the week, it happens.

Let me know if Friday shifts too, otherwise I'll assume it's steady as planned.

subject: Re: Schedule check — Mom's appointments
snippet: Friday's steady, no changes there. Thanks for being flexible on all this, I know...
body:
Friday's steady, no changes there. Thanks for being flexible on all this, I know the bakery schedule isn't exactly forgiving either. I owe you a coffee run at minimum.

Okay, I think we're all set for the week!

subject: Family vacation planning
snippet: I was looking at the calendar and the last week of August seems like the only re...
body:
I was looking at the calendar and the last week of August seems like the only real gap before your busy fall catering season kicks in. Should we start looking at the beach house again, or try somewhere new this year?

Want to lock in dates soon before decent places get booked up.

subject: Re: Family vacation planning
snippet: Last week of August works on my end too, I'll ask Jake to run the shop solo that...
body:
Last week of August works on my end too, I'll ask Jake to run the shop solo that week since he's been wanting more responsibility anyway. I'm honestly fine with the beach house again, Ryan and Emily loved it and it's easy to book.

Go ahead and check availability, I trust your pick either way.

subject: Weekend plans
snippet: Any thoughts on this weekend? I was thinking we could finally do that farmers ma...
body:
Any thoughts on this weekend? I was thinking we could finally do that farmers market Saturday morning before it gets too hot, then maybe just a low-key evening at home. Feels like we haven't had an unscheduled weekend in a while.

Open to other ideas too if you'd rather do something else.

subject: Re: Weekend plans
snippet: Farmers market sounds perfect actually, I could use some inspiration for the sho...
body:
Farmers market sounds perfect actually, I could use some inspiration for the shop anyway, always good to see what other vendors are doing. Low-key evening after works great too, I'm honestly a little wiped from this week.

Should we invite Karen along to the market or keep it low-key?

subject: Re: Weekend plans
snippet: Let's keep it just us this time, we can always do Karen next weekend. I'll pack ...
body:
Let's keep it just us this time, we can always do Karen next weekend. I'll pack a small cooler bag so we're not rushing home right after. Saturday morning it is then.

Looking forward to a slow one for once.

subject: Supplier invoice
snippet: Hey, I was reconciling the bakery accounts tonight and noticed the flour supplie...
body:
Hey, I was reconciling the bakery accounts tonight and noticed the flour supplier billed us twice for the same delivery on the 14th — once under invoice #4471 and again under #4483. Same amount, same order number. Can you check the account before we pay it again?

I didn't want to just ignore it in case it's actually two separate orders, but the dates line up too perfectly to be a coincidence.

subject: Re: Supplier invoice
snippet: Good catch. I pulled up the delivery log and it's definitely a duplicate — only ...
body:
Good catch. I pulled up the delivery log and it's definitely a duplicate — only one truck came that day. I'll call their billing office tomorrow morning and get #4483 voided before it hits the account.

Thanks for staying on top of this, you always catch the stuff I'd miss.

subject: Staffing schedule
snippet: David, quick heads up — Maria asked to swap off this Saturday for a family thing...
body:
David, quick heads up — Maria asked to swap off this Saturday for a family thing, and I don't have anyone else trained on the early proofing shift. Can we shuffle the schedule or do you want me to just cover it myself?

Let me know by tomorrow so I can post the updated board for the week.

subject: Re: Staffing schedule
snippet: Don't cover it yourself, you need the day off too. Let's move Danny to Saturday ...
body:
Don't cover it yourself, you need the day off too. Let's move Danny to Saturday morning since he shadowed you last month, and push Maria to next Tuesday's closing shift instead to even things out.

Go ahead and post it once Danny confirms he's good with the early start.

subject: Re: Staffing schedule
snippet: Danny's confirmed, he's actually excited to run the proofer solo. Schedule's pos...
body:
Danny's confirmed, he's actually excited to run the proofer solo. Schedule's posted for the week. I'll keep an eye on him Saturday morning just in case but I think he'll be fine.

Thanks for sorting it out fast.

subject: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Reminder that the Q2 estimated tax payment is due on the 15th. I ran the numbers...
body:
Reminder that the Q2 estimated tax payment is due on the 15th. I ran the numbers off the bakery's P&L through May and it looks like we should send around $4,200 to stay ahead of underpayment penalties. Can you confirm that matches what you're seeing?

I want to submit it a few days early this time instead of scrambling at the deadline like last quarter.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: That tracks with my numbers too, maybe a touch higher since May was stronger tha...
body:
That tracks with my numbers too, maybe a touch higher since May was stronger than April. I'd say round up to $4,400 just to be safe, especially with the new mixer purchase changing our depreciation this year.

Go ahead and submit whenever you're ready, I trust your math more than mine anyway.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Good point on the mixer, I forgot that would affect this quarter's deduction. On...
body:
Good point on the mixer, I forgot that would affect this quarter's deduction. One more question — should we count the catering deposits we've collected for July, or hold those until next quarter since the work hasn't happened yet?

Want to make sure we're not overstating income for a job we haven't delivered.

subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate
snippet: Hold those until next quarter, the accountant said last year that deposits count...
body:
Hold those until next quarter, the accountant said last year that deposits count when the service is rendered, not when cash comes in. So $4,400 for this round sounds right.

Thanks for double checking, this is exactly the kind of thing I'd forget.

subject: Customer review reply
snippet: We got a rough one-star review this morning saying the sourdough was underbaked ...
body:
We got a rough one-star review this morning saying the sourdough was underbaked and the person waited 20 minutes at the counter. I don't recognize the order but wanted to loop you in before I respond publicly. How do you want to handle it?

Here's the review text if you want to see it before I draft anything.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Let's respond quickly and own it without getting defensive. Something like: 'We'...
body:
Let's respond quickly and own it without getting defensive. Something like: 'We're sorry your visit didn't meet expectations — underbaked loaves and long waits aren't the norm here. We'd love to make it right, please email us directly.' Feel free to tweak the tone.

Also check if we had a wait-staff callout that morning, that might explain the delay.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: We did, actually — Priya called in sick and we were short one register person un...
body:
We did, actually — Priya called in sick and we were short one register person until 10am. I like your draft, maybe just soften 'aren't the norm here' to 'isn't what we aim for' so it doesn't sound defensive either.

Want me to post it as-is with that change?

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Yes, that's better, go ahead and post it. If they email us directly, offer a gif...
body:
Yes, that's better, go ahead and post it. If they email us directly, offer a gift card for a fresh loaf and an apology — no need to ask me first on that, just use your judgment.

Good catch on the tone, that's the kind of thing that turns a bad review into a repeat customer.

subject: Re: Customer review reply
snippet: Posted it. Already got a reply notification, sounds like the review might be one...
body:
Posted it. Already got a reply notification, sounds like the review might be one of ours since the customer mentioned Priya by name in the response thread — small world. I offered the gift card, should hear back soon.

Will update you if it turns into anything worth following up on.

subject: Lunch this week?
snippet: It's been way too long since we grabbed lunch! I've got a light week at the shop...
body:
It's been way too long since we grabbed lunch! I've got a light week at the shop — Wednesday or Thursday work for you? There's that new noodle place on Charles Street I've been dying to try.

No pressure if the bakery's slammed, just say the word.

subject: Re: Lunch this week?
snippet: Thursday works great, Jake's got the shop covered that afternoon. Noodle place s...
body:
Thursday works great, Jake's got the shop covered that afternoon. Noodle place sounds perfect, I've heard good things. Let's do 12:30?

Can't wait to catch up, it really has been too long.

subject: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Hey stranger, I know Tuesdays are usually your slower day at the bakery. Want to...
body:
Hey stranger, I know Tuesdays are usually your slower day at the bakery. Want to grab coffee at that place near the flower shop around 9am before we both get swallowed by the day? I miss our chats.

Let me know if that time's no good and we'll find another.

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: 9am works, I'll swing by after I get the shop opened up. Honestly could use the break...
body:
9am works, I'll swing by after I get the shop opened up. Honestly could use the break, it's been a chaotic couple weeks between the schedule shuffle and a new supplier mess.

See you there, save me a seat by the window.

subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?
snippet: Window seat secured. Bring the supplier drama, I've got plenty of flower deliver...
body:
Window seat secured. Bring the supplier drama, I've got plenty of flower delivery horror stories to trade. It'll be good to actually sit down instead of texting between errands.

See you Tuesday!

subject: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Okay I'm not letting your birthday slide by quietly this year. I was thinking br...
body:
Okay I'm not letting your birthday slide by quietly this year. I was thinking brunch on Saturday at that place with the bottomless mimosas you liked last summer — I can handle the reservation if you just tell me a rough headcount.

Also tell me if the kids are coming, changes whether I book the patio or the quieter back room.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: You're too sweet for this, honestly a quiet coffee would've been fine. Kids will...
body:
You're too sweet for this, honestly a quiet coffee would've been fine. Kids will be there for the first hour then heading to my sister's, so maybe the back room works better early and we shift outside once they leave.

Headcount's probably eight, I'll confirm once I hear back from a couple people.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Back room to patio transition, got it, I'll ask if they can hold both. Eight sou...
body:
Back room to patio transition, got it, I'll ask if they can hold both. Eight sounds manageable for the reservation. Should I order a cake or are we keeping it low-key with just brunch food?

Also no arguing about the bill, this one's on me.

subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans
snippet: Fine, fine, I'll let you win this one. Skip the cake, we'll have plenty of pastr...
body:
Fine, fine, I'll let you win this one. Skip the cake, we'll have plenty of pastries from the shop that I can bring instead — least I can do. Saturday at 11 then?

Really, thank you for putting this together, means a lot.
